description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dark fuselage and dual wings stacked one above the other, connected by struts and wires. The aircraft features a single propeller at the nose, a rounded cockpit canopy, and a tail section adorned with a tricolor pattern of red, white, and green. Distinctive insignia are visible on the upper wings and fuselage sides, including a white cross and circular emblems. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, all fixed and exposed. The overall design suggests a classic military or training aircraft, with a robust metal body and minimalistic, functional detailing.
description: A dark-colored vintage biplane with dual stacked wings, a single nose propeller, and a tricolor tail. Markings and insignia are visible on the wings and fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the cockpit, fuselage insignia, and tricolor tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail section, tricolor rudder, and the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view reveals the cockpit, fuselage markings, and the propeller profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small fixed tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a rounded canopy.
